prompt: What is the core weakness in this login logic?
code_snippet: if (username != null) { login(username); }   // password is never checked

options.text: The password is hashed with a weak algorithm
The user lacks permission for a specific resource (an authorisation problem)
Authentication is improperly implemented — identity is not actually verified, so anyone can authenticate

explanation: The code lets a caller through without genuinely verifying who they are (no real password/token check) — Improper Authentication (CWE-287). This is distinct from authorisation (CWE-862), which is about what an already-identified user may do.
source_reference: CWE-287; OWASP A07:2021 (Identification and Authentication Failures)

if (ok) login();   // debug bypass left enabled
